heuristicNotes: Internal label count is a curated estimate for instructional labels. Repeated token chips are treated as examples, not separate explanatory labels.
Generated-image checks rely on the current visual type and selected template from this readiness registry.
Screenshot presence is checked by audit:visual-aids after screenshots are captured.

templates.name: Atmospheric Scene
templates.purpose: Big-picture, humane, social, ethical, or metaphorical concepts.

templates.name: Context Tray / Stack
templates.purpose: What is visible now, what falls out, and what retrieval adds.

templates.name: Taxonomy Map
templates.purpose: Relationships among families, layers, risks, benefits, or actors.

topWatchList.whyWatch: Distributed side may feel abstract; tester feedback should guide simplification.
Axis labels must stay outside crowded cells.
Validation markers must remain readable at 320px.
Long token rows must wrap without horizontal overflow.
Avoid adding feature names to the bars.
Keep block text short; no layer-by-layer story.
Keep embedding and hidden-state labels visibly distinct.
Avoid morality imagery that makes the model seem conscious.
Later atmospheric image could improve warmth, but current coded flow is readable.
Later atmospheric image could improve tone; no numbers should be added.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Atmospheric hero supports the opening idea; exact mechanics are carried by HTML callouts.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Recognize an LLM as a learned next-token prediction system rather than a mind, database, or rulebook.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Context enters, learned weights shape probabilities, and one response token is generated.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: An LLM is a conscious reader or live database.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The first card needs a humane big-picture opening, not a crowded mechanism diagram.
calloutsBelowVisual: One token is generated and appended.
Ordinary inference does not rewrite weights.
briefs.altText: A prompt entering a glowing folded-paper feature cloud before one response token emerges.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. A prompt ribbon enters a crystalline model cloud and exits as one small token glow.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep the PNG textless; all mechanism language must stay in HTML.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the opening visual reduces mystery without implying hidden powers.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Readable taxonomy; keep definitions below rather than adding dense labels.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Place LLMs inside AI, machine learning, deep learning, and generative AI without treating those terms as synonyms.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: LLMs are one branch inside a larger AI family.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: All AI is an LLM, or all generative AI works like ChatGPT.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The lesson is a family/category map.
calloutsBelowVisual: Machine learning learns patterns from data.
briefs.altText: A compact AI family tree showing LLMs as one branch of generative AI.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Folded branch tree with short node labels and one active branch explanation below.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Do not add more branch definitions inside the tree.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the map prevents using AI, ML, generative AI, and LLM interchangeably.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Comparison is clear; loss is supported by glossary and callout.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Compare rules-first systems with learned-pattern systems and hybrids.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Explicit rules and learned examples are different traditions that modern systems may combine.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Deep learning is just a giant hand-written rulebook.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The core is a boundary between two traditions.
calloutsBelowVisual: Learned systems use examples, loss, and weights.
briefs.altText: Two panels compare rule-based AI with learned-pattern systems and a hybrid bridge.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two-column board plus bridge, with loss explained below.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep loss explanation in callout, not as a paragraph inside the board.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Watch whether learners understand loss as a training signal.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: The durable weight-update step is visible and numbered.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Trace training as prediction, loss, and durable weight updates.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Training changes weights through repeated prediction-error updates.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: A normal prompt trains the model permanently.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Training is a step sequence.
briefs.altText: A five-step training loop with weight update highlighted as durable change.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Five numbered loop nodes with update weights visually emphasized.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Five is the maximum; do not add side notes inside the loop.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether update weights is the clearly durable step.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Atmospheric scale works; exact training details remain in callouts.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain pretraining as broad weight-shaping from many examples, not perfect source recall.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Many examples shape durable weights into broad reusable patterns.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Pretraining stores a perfect copy of sources.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The learner needs scale and humane calm more than a dense pipeline.
briefs.altText: A broad folded-paper landscape suggesting many training examples shaping a model before use.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. Wide folded landscape with many small paper streams flowing toward a calm model horizon.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid implying source documents are individually retrieved from weights.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the visual suggests scale without memorization.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Chart is testable but should be watched for label readability and validation-example clarity.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Distinguish memorizing training examples from learning patterns that transfer to set-aside validation examples.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: A smoother pattern can transfer better than a curve that hugs old training dots.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Doing well on training examples proves generalization.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It compares overfit and generalizing behavior in one plot.
calloutsBelowVisual: Generalizing curve reaches new examples better.
briefs.altText: A plot comparing a jagged overfit curve with a smoother generalizing curve and set-aside validation examples.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: One chart with four markers and HTML callouts; no extra legend text.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Validation markers must remain readable at 320px.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether set-aside validation examples are clear.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Rebuilt from generated metaphor into a coded comparison board separating durable adaptation from current-run steering.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Separate durable fine-tuning or adapters from prompt, RAG, and sampling behavior during one run.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Fine-tuning can shape future behavior; prompt/RAG/sampling steer the current run.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Prompting once is the same as fine-tuning.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The core is a boundary between durable adaptation and temporary/current-run mechanisms.
calloutsBelowVisual: Fine-tuning can durably adapt future behavior.
briefs.altText: A four-column comparison board separates fine-tuning from prompt steering, RAG context, and sampling.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Four compact comparison columns with numbered callouts below.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep current-run details in callouts rather than adding more labels.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether durable/current-run boundary is clear after five seconds.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Textless metaphor is suitable; conscience boundary is in callouts.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain alignment as behavior shaping, feedback, policy, and guardrails, not conscience.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Alignment changes behavior patterns or system controls without giving moral understanding.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Alignment gives the model a conscience.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Alignment is a broad behavior-shaping landscape, not one exact mechanism.
calloutsBelowVisual: Feedback can guide training or evaluation.
The model does not gain conscience.
briefs.altText: A folded garden landscape representing behavior shaping and safer paths without text.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. Folded-paper garden paths with soft guardrail-like light rails, no text, no robot face.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid morality imagery that makes the model seem conscious.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the visual feels like behavior shaping rather than moral awakening.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Fixed weights and temporary activations are visually separated.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Trace inference as a forward pass using fixed weights and temporary internal states.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Inference uses weights but normally does not update them.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Every response permanently teaches the model.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Inference is a step flow from context to token.
calloutsBelowVisual: Weights are used, not normally updated.
briefs.altText: Current context flows through fixed weights and temporary activations toward next-token scores and a token.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Four numbered markers with fixed weights visually separate from temporary activations.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Do not add more state names inside the diagram.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether fixed versus temporary is visible.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Recent stacked layout is readable and testable.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Separate given prompt tokens from generated response tokens and the updated context for the next run.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Prompt is provided; response tokens are generated and appended.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The model writes the whole answer at once.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It is a four-step run trace.
briefs.altText: Four stacked panels show prompt, response so far, next token, and updated context.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: HTML cards with stable vertical spacing and wrapping token chips.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep panel padding compact and tokens wrapped.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the fourth updated-context row is clear.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: HTML token rows make uneven chunks readable after prior repair.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show that tokens can be words, pieces, punctuation, or other chunks.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Text becomes uneven model-readable chunks.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Every token is exactly one word.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Tokenization is a transformation from text to chunks.
calloutsBelowVisual: Punctuation can be its own token.
briefs.altText: A sentence becomes token chips, then uneven examples split a word and punctuation.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: HTML cards with source, token stream, and uneven examples.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Long token rows must wrap without horizontal overflow.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether floor plus punctuation is understandable.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Lookup chain is direct and marker/callout mapping passes.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Separate token text, numeric token ID, and embedding-table lookup row.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Token IDs are lookup keys, not meaning.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The number itself contains the meaning.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It shows token to ID to table row.
briefs.altText: Token cards connect to numeric ID cards and then to embedding table rows.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Three-row lookup chain with four numbered callouts.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep numeric labels large enough.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether ID versus meaning is clear.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Durable table and temporary retrieval boundary is visible.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain embedding as a token’s learned starting vector retrieved from a durable table.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: A token ID retrieves a learned starting vector.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Embedding means a dictionary definition or a hidden memory.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It is a lookup process.
calloutsBelowVisual: The table was learned during training.
The current run retrieves a row.
That row is a starting vector.
briefs.altText: A token ID points into a durable table and retrieves a temporary starting vector.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: ID card, table, selected row, and downstream hidden-state note.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid making the table look like searchable source memory.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether durable table versus temporary vector is clear.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Teaching sliders are useful; distributed features may still look abstract and should be watched.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain vectors as lists of numbers with distributed features rather than neat English meanings per dimension.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Many fuzzy numerical features vary at once.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Each vector dimension has a simple human label.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It compares teaching sliders with unlabeled distributed features.
briefs.altText: Labeled teaching sliders are contrasted with unlabeled distributed numerical bars.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two-panel board; remove extra dots in a later pass if testers find them decorative.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Distributed side may feel abstract; tester feedback should guide simplification.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the distributed side helps or just looks decorative.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Readable enough, but batch sheet and axes should be watched on 320px.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show tensors as shaped numerical blocks that organize many vectors.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: A tensor can arrange token positions and feature dimensions together.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: A tensor is a mysterious object rather than a shaped block of numbers.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The visual maps axes and grouped numerical structure.
briefs.altText: A grid block shows token rows, feature columns, and a faint batch sheet.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Simple grid with two axes and one optional batch layer.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Axis labels must stay outside crowded cells.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether the batch sheet clarifies or distracts.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: v0.27.18 rebuild is readable; watch arc endpoints on 320px.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain attention as weighted relevance between token positions, not awareness.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: The target token uses one source clue more strongly than another.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Attention means human attention or consciousness.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It contrasts stronger and weaker relevance clues around one target.
briefs.altText: Sentence chips show it connected strongly to cat and weakly to dog by relevance arcs.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Six token chips with one strong arc and one faint arc; no dense labels.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Arc endpoints must remain visually attached to chips.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether not-awareness is clear without clutter.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Readable enough for testing after label-size repair.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Contrast attention mixing across token positions with MLP reshaping features within each position.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: The MLP reshapes one token position’s features.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The MLP is another cross-token attention step or human reasoning center.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The lesson is a boundary between attention and MLP.
calloutsBelowVisual: The token position stays the same.
briefs.altText: Attention mixing is contrasted with a cat token entering an MLP and leaving with reshaped feature bars.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two-panel comparison with one token position and before/after bars.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid adding feature names to the bars.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether attention versus MLP is separable.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Stack is compact and testable.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show repeated transformer blocks refining temporary hidden states without implying conscious thinking steps.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Layers repeat attention and MLP transformations.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Layers are human-like thought steps or permanent memory slots.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It is repeated flow through blocks.
calloutsBelowVisual: Each block applies attention and MLP.
briefs.altText: Input hidden states move through repeated transformer blocks toward refined states.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Three stacked blocks with one output rail.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep block text short; no layer-by-layer story.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it feels mechanical rather than human-thought-like.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Temporary state flow is acceptable after label repair.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Contrast learned starting embeddings with temporary context-shaped hidden states.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Hidden states are temporary vectors shaped by context during the run.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Hidden states are durable memory or citations.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It shows state transformation through layers.
briefs.altText: A token starts as an embedding and becomes temporary hidden states through layers.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Short flow from embedding to layers to hidden state, with durable weights separated.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep embedding and hidden-state labels visibly distinct.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether hidden state sounds temporary, not memory.
briefs.recommendation: Accept for small human testing; improve in a later visual-design pass.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Shared token set and bars are clear.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain logits as raw next-token scores before probability conversion.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Candidate next tokens receive raw scores.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: A high logit is already a probability or truth score.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Raw scores are best seen as bars before softmax.
briefs.altText: Raw score bars compare floor, mat, kitchen, and banana before probability conversion.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Horizontal or vertical raw-score bars using consistent candidate tokens.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Do not add decimals too small to read.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether raw-score versus probability is clear.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Probability conversion is clear and separated from selection.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show softmax turning raw scores into probabilities that sum to one.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Scores become a probability distribution.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Softmax chooses the token or verifies truth.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Probabilities are best shown as normalized bars.
briefs.altText: Candidate tokens become probability bars that sum to one after softmax.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Same tokens as Logits with normalized bars and total cue.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep percentages readable and avoid truth-meter styling.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether learners see that softmax does not choose.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: v0.27.18 bar layout fixed previous crowding.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain sampling as choosing one next token from probability-shaped options.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: One token is selected from a probability distribution.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The most likely token is guaranteed true or always selected.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Sampling depends on probability-shaped options.
calloutsBelowVisual: Less likely tokens can still appear.
briefs.altText: Probability bars lead to one selected next-token card.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Readable bars plus one selected-token card below.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Selected token must not collide with bars.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether likely-not-true lands.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Numbered repeat cycle is readable.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Trace autoregressive generation as choose one token, append it, and run again.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Generation repeats one token at a time.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The model writes the whole answer in one hidden burst.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It is a looped sequence.
calloutsBelowVisual: Response so far is visible context.
briefs.altText: A four-step loop shows one token chosen, appended, and fed into the next run.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Four numbered nodes with return arrow.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep loop arrow obvious without crossing text.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether append-repeat is clear.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Bounded tray is clear.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain context window as temporary visible input, not permanent memory.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Only visible context is available; old context can fall out.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Context window is permanent memory.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The concept is exactly a bounded temporary tray.
calloutsBelowVisual: The next run uses what remains visible.
briefs.altText: A bounded context tray shows cards inside, capacity, and older cards falling out.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Tray with entering/current/falling-out cards.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Cards must remain readable without many words.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether context versus memory is repaired.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Open-book retrieval flow is clear and numbered.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain RAG as retrieval plus temporary context, not training or permanent memory.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Retrieved snippets are inserted into current context before generation.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: RAG means the model permanently learned the documents or searched by itself.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: RAG adds outside cards to the current tray.
calloutsBelowVisual: User prompt starts the search step.
Response is still generated token by token.
briefs.altText: A prompt sends a retriever to notes, then retrieved cards enter a context tray before response generation.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Five-step retrieval-to-context flow.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep retriever and model visually separate.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether RAG is not training.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Claim-to-evidence support map is readable.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain grounding as connecting claims to available evidence without guaranteeing truth.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: A claim is stronger when it is supported by matching evidence.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: A citation-looking answer is automatically true.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Grounding connects claims to evidence cards.
briefs.altText: Generated claims connect to evidence cards, with one missing support line marked for review.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Claim cards, evidence cards, support lines, and review marker.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid too many source-card labels.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether grounding is helpful but not a truth guarantee.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Support/missing-support metaphor is testable.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain hallucination as fluent generated output outrunning support, not intentional lying.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Fluent output can contain claims with weak or missing support.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: A hallucination means the model is lying on purpose.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The core contrast is supported versus unsupported claims.
briefs.altText: A smooth output bridge has one supported claim and one unsupported span needing review.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Bridge with support pillars and a missing support marker.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep bridge metaphor from looking like a guarantee.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether hallucination differs from lying.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Simplified from dense matrix into four mode cards: training, fine-tune, RAG, and prompt.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Compare durable learning, current-run steering, retrieval, and evaluation without treating all useful behavior as training.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Different system changes happen at different times and places.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Every useful answer means the model learned permanently.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The lesson compares nearby modes and asks what changed.
briefs.altText: Four mode cards compare durable training and fine-tuning with current-run RAG and prompting.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two-column board: durable changes on one side and current-run steering on the other.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Watch whether learners distinguish evaluation from the four visual modes using callouts.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether learners can say which modes change weights.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Two-lane contrast is clear.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Distinguish autoregressive token generation from diffusion-style denoising.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Autoregression appends tokens; diffusion refines noise.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: All generative AI works like ChatGPT.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: Two mechanisms are compared side by side.
calloutsBelowVisual: Text grows one token at a time.
Both generate outputs but differ mechanically.
briefs.altText: One lane appends text tokens while another lane denoises a noisy pattern into an output.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two lanes with three short steps each.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid image-like details that distract from mechanism contrast.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether diffusion and autoregression feel distinct.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Reduced from many media inputs/outputs to three lanes flowing through shared representation and output.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain multimodal AI as systems that process or represent multiple media types together.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Text, image, and audio can connect through learned representations or linked components.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Multimodal means human-like perception.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It maps media families into a shared representation area.
calloutsBelowVisual: Systems encode media into usable representations.
briefs.altText: Three media lanes for text, image, and audio connect through a shared representation area to an output.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Three short input lanes, one shared-space node, one output node.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Video and code are explained in callouts rather than added as extra labels.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it teaches multimodal without implying human senses.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Convergence layout remains readable, with short ingredient labels and caption below the diagram.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Explain modern LLM capability as convergence, not one unexplained event.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Data, compute, storage, methods, labor, and incentives converged.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: One spark or one algorithm explains modern LLMs.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The visual groups conditions converging into one capability node.
calloutsBelowVisual: Compute and storage made scale practical.
Labor and incentives moved systems into products.
briefs.altText: Six ingredients converge into a modern LLM capability node.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Six short ingredient chips converge into one capability node.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Do not put the convergence caption inside the diagram.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether convergence, not spark, is obvious.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Simplified to human work -> training data -> model patterns -> provenance questions.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show that model capability depends on human-created traces while rights and provenance remain human questions.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Human expression powers patterns; accountability remains human.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: The model created its abilities alone.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The visual maps provenance and model-pattern relationships without cramming source categories.
calloutsBelowVisual: Provenance, consent, copyright, and compensation matter.
The model absorbs patterns without responsibility.
briefs.altText: A simple flow connects human work to training data, model patterns, and provenance questions.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. A lantern lit by many folded paper pages, code sheets, art fragments, and research notes; contemplative, no text.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Four-node provenance flow with source details in HTML callouts.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could improve warmth, but current coded flow is readable.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether it raises provenance without feeling accusatory or decorative.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Simplified into five cost categories with no fake statistics or cramped dashboard.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Make physical, social, cultural, and ethical costs visible without exaggeration.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Instant answers have infrastructure and institutional costs.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Digital systems are weightless or cost-free.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The card groups categories that must be counted rather than charting false precision.
calloutsBelowVisual: Infrastructure costs vary by system and workload.
Power and governance shape who benefits.
briefs.altText: An AI answer connects to five cost categories: compute, energy and water, labor, privacy, and institutional risk.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. A small glowing answer card connected to hidden folded-paper infrastructure, cooling, labor, privacy, and governance symbols; no numbers, no labels.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Five-category ledger map with cautious HTML callouts.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could improve tone; no numbers should be added.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether costs feel countable without fearmongering.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Two-column ledger is appropriate.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Separate practical mechanism-based risks from myths about consciousness, omniscience, or hidden model powers.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Real risks come from data, context, tools, and institutions.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Risk mainly means sci-fi doom or conscious chatbots.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: It sorts risk categories and myths into clear groups.
calloutsBelowVisual: Privacy, hallucination, tools, and overreliance are real mechanisms.
Conscious bots and secret self-training are myths.
briefs.altText: A two-column ledger sorts practical mechanism risks from unsupported myths.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Two-column ledger with short labels and HTML details.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Avoid listing too many risks inside the visual.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether risk feels practical rather than spooky.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Simplified to bounded benefit chips with human review rather than a dense tier chart.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Separate real bounded benefits from speculative hype while preserving human review.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Benefits can be real, useful, and still bounded.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Taking benefits seriously requires utopian hype.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The card groups bounded use cases around review.
calloutsBelowVisual: Some uses are useful now with review.
briefs.altText: Bounded benefit chips for drafting, translation, summarization, access, and review sit inside a calm benefit map.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. A calm tool garden with accessibility, translation, search, drafting, and review symbols arranged around a human path; no text.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Five short benefit chips plus a review note.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could add warmth, but current coded visual is readable.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether benefits feel bounded, not hype.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Recentered human judgment and made review, appeal, and governance visible.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Keep dignity, judgment, governance, and accountability central in AI deployment.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: AI output should support people inside accountable decision contexts.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: If AI is powerful, it should decide.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The visual maps actors and accountability paths.
calloutsBelowVisual: The model can sound ethical without moral understanding.
briefs.altText: Human judgment sits at the center while an AI tool supports, people are affected, and review, appeal, and governance remain visible.
briefs.image2Prompt: ZenTron Origami style, folded paper forms, subtle neon cyan and violet light, humane academic tone, no embedded text, no labels, no charts, no photorealistic identifiable people, mobile-first composition. Human judgment at the center: folded paper people silhouettes around a decision table, small AI support note, governance light rails, no robot, no text.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Human judgment center with AI tool, people, review, appeal, and governance nodes.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Later atmospheric image could improve warmth, but current coded flow keeps accountability clear.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether human responsibility is visually central.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Reduced dense lever panel to data, evaluation, policy, human review, and incentives.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show that outcomes are shaped by design, deployment, governance, and incentives.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Better AI depends on human choices about data, evaluation, policy, review, and incentives.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Harms are inevitable if the technology is useful.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The card maps a small set of decision levers and actors.
calloutsBelowVisual: Technical choices can fit the task.
Institutional review and evaluation shape outcomes.
briefs.altText: A compact better-AI lever panel shows data, evaluation, policy, human review, and incentives.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Five-lever grouped-node map.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep mitigation details in callouts, not inside the lever panel.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask which lever group they remember after five seconds.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Context-tray metaphor is strong and aligned with the Journey.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Show effective prompting as context design for one run, not permanent teaching.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Prompt parts are packed into the current context tray.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Prompting is special wording or fine-tuning.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: The visual is literally a current-context tray.
calloutsBelowVisual: Prompting usually changes context, not weights.
briefs.altText: Prompt component cards drop into a transparent current context tray before one generated response leaves it.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Cards entering bounded tray; response exits from tray.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Keep card labels short and avoid overpacking the tray.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask whether prompting feels temporary.

briefs.currentIssueSummary: Split dense capstone map into three lanes: mechanics, evidence, and responsibility.
briefs.oneSentenceLearningObjective: Connect mechanics, evidence, review, and human accountability into a durable mental model.
briefs.oneConceptVisualMustConvey: Mechanics matter, and humans remain responsible.
briefs.misconceptionVisualMustCorrect: Fluency equals understanding or wisdom.
briefs.whyThisTemplateFits: This final card maps three families of model literacy rather than every individual mechanism.
calloutsBelowVisual: Prompt becomes tokens, states, scores, probabilities, and sampled response tokens.
RAG and grounding can add evidence but not truth guarantees.
Benefits, costs, review, governance, and accountability remain human responsibilities.
briefs.altText: A three-lane synthesis map groups model mechanics, evidence checking, and human responsibility.
briefs.codedLayoutSketch: Three simple lanes with no more than three labels each.
briefs.mobileRiskNotes: Ask testers if the capstone feels too abstract after simplification.
briefs.notesForHumanTesters: Ask what one idea they retain from the capstone visual.
